summary: Matched the ftCommon fighter helper `ftCo_800A5A5C` as a boolean predicate over `Fighter.motion_id`, returning true for four death-related common action states. The PR also corrected the function prototype from `void` to `bool` in the header and refactored asm-differ objdump path selection into a reusable `find_objdump()` helper that respects `PPC_EABI_OBJDUMP` before falling back to devkitPPC.
what_changed: `ftCo_800A5A5C` was implemented in `ftCo_0A01.c`, replacing the decomp placeholder comment.
The implementation takes a `Fighter* fp` and switches on `fp->motion_id`.
The function returns `true` for `ftCo_MS_DeadLeft`, `ftCo_MS_DeadRight`, `ftCo_MS_DeadUpStar`, and `ftCo_MS_DeadUpStarIce`; otherwise it returns `false`.
The declaration in `ftCo_0A01.h` changed from `void ftCo_800A5A5C(ftCo_Fighter*)` to `bool ftCo_800A5A5C(ftCo_Fighter*)`, aligning the header with the matched predicate behavior.

`find_objdump()` first checks the `PPC_EABI_OBJDUMP` environment variable, then falls back to constructing the devkitPPC `powerpc-eabi-objdump` path using `DEVKITPRO`, `DEVKITPPC`, and `get_exe_suffix()`.
`apply()` now assigns `config["objdump_executable"] = find_objdump()` instead of inlining the devkitPPC path construction.
smart_moves: Expressed `ftCo_800A5A5C` as a compact switch over named motion-state enum constants rather than using raw numeric IDs.
Used early boolean returns in the switch, which cleanly models the function as a membership test for specific death states.
Corrected the public prototype to `bool`, avoiding a stale `void` declaration that would mislead future callers and decomp work.
Preserved the existing `ftCo_Fighter*` typedef in the header while using `Fighter*` in the C implementation, consistent with surrounding fighter code patterns visible in the diff.
Factored objdump discovery into a named helper, making asm-differ configuration easier to override and reuse.
Added `PPC_EABI_OBJDUMP` override support, which makes local matching workflows less dependent on a fixed devkitPPC install path.
decomp_lessons: Small unknown fighter helpers may simply be boolean predicates over `motion_id`; check whether the asm corresponds to a small set-membership test over action-state constants.
When matching a function that returns a boolean, update both the implementation and header prototype; stale `void` declarations are a decomp hazard.
Named motion-state enums such as `ftCo_MS_DeadLeft` and `ftCo_MS_DeadUpStarIce` improve searchability and reduce ambiguity compared with raw action-state IDs.
For common-state helper functions, a switch with grouped cases can be the clearest source form for matching a chain of comparisons against motion IDs.
Tooling fixes that improve local diff workflows, such as configurable objdump discovery, can be bundled with matching work when they directly support verification.
naming_conventions: `ftCo_800A5A5C` follows the project convention of address-based names for not-yet-semantic ftCommon functions.
Common fighter motion-state constants use the `ftCo_MS_` prefix, as seen in `ftCo_MS_DeadLeft`, `ftCo_MS_DeadRight`, `ftCo_MS_DeadUpStar`, and `ftCo_MS_DeadUpStarIce`.
Fighter common object typedefs in the header include names such as `ftCo_Fighter*` and `ftCo_GObj*`, while implementation code may use the underlying `Fighter*` type.
The asm-differ helper name `find_objdump()` describes discovery rather than configuration mutation, keeping it reusable.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched the function with a switch statement over `fp->motion_id`, grouping all true-returning cases together and using `default` for false.
The matched predicate likely corresponds to assembly comparing `motion_id` against a small set of death-state IDs, though the PR context does not include the assembly itself.
Changing the function return type to `bool` was part of the match: the implementation returns true/false and the header now reflects that.
The tooling change lets asm-differ use `PPC_EABI_OBJDUMP` directly, which can help contributors compare output when their binutils are not located under the default devkitPPC path.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
There is no explicit reviewer rationale in the slice; conclusions are based on the title, changed files, and diff excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Find callers of `ftCo_800A5A5C` to determine the semantic name for this death-state predicate.
Search for nearby unmatched functions `ftCo_800A5A28` and `ftCo_800A5A90` in `ftCo_0A01.c` for related common-state logic.
Audit other headers for functions still declared `void` where matched implementations return `bool`.
Check whether other tooling configs should use the same `PPC_EABI_OBJDUMP` override or `find_objdump()` pattern.
Look for additional predicates over `fp->motion_id` that can be matched using grouped switch cases over `ftCo_MS_*` constants.
